subject: PluralSync 🔄 - Added PluralKit as Syncing Source 💖
body:
Dear PluralSync Users,

Being able to synchronize from PluralKit to other platforms and system managers was a frequently requested feature from the beginning of PluralSync.

            We're happy to announce, that from now on, you can sync Pluralkit -> * ! ❤️

Check the settings to configure the synchronisation. The synchronisation respects the privacy levels of the fields and the member visibility - and it can be configured as well.
This integration is still fresh - so issues might arise. If you have issues or have feedback, let us know on the community server!
Due to technical details, the requests against PluralKit are slowed down a bit - but this should be resolved in a few weeks and not be noticeable for you practically.

Given the near-future discontinuation of SimplyPlural ( https://apparyllis.com/simply-plural-will-be-discontinued/ ), having more options on PluralSync is important.

We're very happy for the PluralKit community and especially the devs for their interactions with us. We enjoy the open-source availability of PluralKit and
that also enabled us to contribute new code to PluralKit which is needed for PluralSync to better support this synchronisation sustainabily.

Thank you for your attenion.

Kinds, PluralSync

subject: Developer Hiatus in June and Possibly Longer
body:
Dear PluralSync Users,

The developer(s) are taking a break from almost all activities due to personal reasons starting June 2026. The break was planned a long time ago already and is not
            related to the current situation regarding plural system apps. The devs will return from anywhere between 2 weeks to 1.5 months.

The Pluralsync servers will be continously running during this duration. There won't be any new features during this break. In the community spaces, non-developers are still
            there and they can help with certain issues if you have any. The servers run stable by themselves without the developers' active hand.
            
Thank you for your attenion.

Kinds, PluralSync

subject: PluralSync 🔄 - Regarding the Discontinuation of SimplyPlural
body:
Dear PluralSync Users,

Unfortunately, recently it was announced that SimplyPlural will be discontinued ( https://apparyllis.com/simply-plural-will-be-discontinued/ ).
We're very sad to hear that and we have deep empathy with the developer(s) - as we're aware of the complexities of maintaining an open source project used by many many people.
We're deeply thankful for the existence of SimplyPlural since that's a core reason PluralSync was created in the first place.

At the same time, we're aware, that many PluralSync users deeply depend on SimplyPlural. In that regards, we want to clarify,
how PluralSync will move in future given the recent announcement.

As the developers of PluralSync, we're watching how the situation will develop.
Perhaps someone will take over and continue the server maintenance for SimplyPlural.
Perhaps the community will move to one or two main alternatives. Or maybe SimplyPlural will become self-hosted by many.

Either way PluralSync will keep on functioning and add integrations so that syncing will keep on working.
Only SimplyPlural-related functionality in PluralSync will stop working once the SP servers are shutdown. 
Since the situation is new and fresh, things might change so this is not a 100% statement on what will happen.
But we can tell you that we're *planning* to continue PluralSync in this way. The new integrations will be whatever the community at large decided to use.
The software behind PluralSync is currently focused on SimplyPlural - but we are planning to separate that. When that will happen depends on our time and energy and we don't make any promises currently.

As developers of PluralSync, we've also taken note of how the community has reacted towards the sudden announcement.
While it's a stressful situation for many, we'd like to remind everybody, that the SimplyPlural developers have poured their heart and soul and money
and stress over a long time for this - and this is also true for the other developers (PluralKit, Octocon, etc.).
Please be kind to such developers. They do these things, because of love - and earn nothing to little from these projects
while taking responsibilities and risks. Please be kind to them and absolutely avoid demanding things - because
at the end of the day, there is no reason for others to expect anything, when developers offer the software and service for FREE (for most users).
A kinder atmosphere will make it easier for software to be further developed - but a harsh atmosphere will move developers away.
Finally, please keep rumors and gossip away from such discussions. Online these days, it's easy to take quick conclusions based on limited data.
Please don't jump to conclusions. It's better to simply note things and be less confident of other peoples malice. Ignorance or genuine misunderstandings are far more commonly the actual cause.


Thanks you and deep respect and gratitude to the developer(s) of SimplyPlural for everything they've done for this community! ❤️
            

Kinds, PluralSync

subject: PluralSync 🔄 Email Announcements to All Users Activated
body:
Dear PluralSync Users,

As of today (2026-03-11), PluralSync will start sending essential announcement emails concerning the service and the users usage.

These emails cannot be deactivated because they're essential.

Any additional information or discussion can be had in the corresponding community spaces (see website footer for discord link).

Thank you for using PluralSync. We kindly appreciate and wish you a pleasent and useful time with it.

Kinds, PluralSync
